systemProperties.eclipse.launcher.name: Knime
systemProperties.org.osgi.framework.executionenvironment: OSGi/Minimum-1.0, OSGi/Minimum-1.1, OSGi/Minimum-1.2, JavaSE/compact1-1.8, JavaSE/compact2-1.8, JavaSE/compact3-1.8, JRE-1.1, J2SE-1.2, J2SE-1.3, J2SE-1.4, J2SE-1.5, JavaSE-1.6, JavaSE-1.7, JavaSE-1.8, JavaSE-9, JavaSE-10, JavaSE-11, JavaSE-12, JavaSE-13, JavaSE-14, JavaSE-15, JavaSE-16, JavaSE-17
systemProperties.java.specification.name: Java Platform API Specification
systemProperties.user.name: massei
systemProperties.java.vm.specification.name: Java Virtual Machine Specification
systemProperties.os.name: Linux
systemProperties.java.runtime.name: OpenJDK Runtime Environment
systemProperties.java.vm.name: OpenJDK 64-Bit Server VM
systemProperties.org.osgi.framework.os.name: Linux

node.name: List Files (deprecated)
node.annotation: Select folder with input images

node.name: Model Reader (deprecated)
node.annotation: Read a saved Model

nodeMessage.message: Failed to extract specification from "knime://knime.workflow/data/DAB stain_AZ_TN Pixel Classification model.zip": Can't load class "org.knime.knip.suise.node.port.WekaClassifierPortObjectSpec" Status: DataLoadError: Model Reader (deprecated) 8:27 Status: DataLoadError: Unable to load port content for node "Model Reader (deprecated)": Invalid spec class "org.knime.knip.suise.node.port.WekaClassifierPortObjectSpec" Status: DataLoadError: State has changed from EXECUTED to IDLE

node.name: Row Sampling
node.annotation: Insert node between Row Splitter and Train/Apply Pixel Classification Model if you run out of memory

node.name: Image Reader (Table)
node.annotation: Open images

node.name: Image Properties
node.annotation: Extract filename for each image

node.name: Splitter
node.annotation: Split images to separate channels

node.name: MISSING Invert...
node.annotation: Invert pixel values

node.name: MISSING Gaussian Blur...
node.annotation: Gaussian Blur with selected sigma radius

node.name: MISSING Image Calculator...
node.annotation: Choose SUBTRACT in operation drop-down Choose image 0 in first, image2 in second setting drop-down

node.name: Row Splitter
node.annotation: Split into training and test images

node.name: Column Filter
node.annotation: Only compute model based on calculated image

node.name: MISSING Fiji Trainable Segmentation Features 2D
node.annotation: Compute feature stack for input images

node.name: Interactive Annotator
node.annotation: Annotate stain and background

node.name: Joiner (deprecated)
node.annotation: Join Label Annotations with Extracted Features

annotations.annotation:
To retrain a model, open the configuration dialog of the Interative Annotator node and label the input images:
1) Select 0 or 1 from the Labels 2) Use a selection tool from the Toolbox to create a selection for the previously selected label

node.name: Column Filter
node.annotation: Only compute model based on calculated image

node.name: MISSING Fiji Trainable Segmentation Features 2D
node.annotation: Compute feature stack for input images

node.name: MISSING Pixel Classification Apply
node.annotation: Apply model to images

node.name: Structuring Element Creator
node.annotation: Sphere element with radius of fixed size

node.name: Image Converter
node.annotation: Normalize and Scale Images

node.name: MISSING Erode
node.annotation: Controlled erosion of Pixel Data

node.name: Connected Component Analysis
node.annotation: Generate Labels

node.name: Labeling Filter
node.annotation: Filter Labels by size

node.name: Morphological Labeling Operations
node.annotation: Dilate Individual Labels with selected element

node.name: Label Transformer
node.annotation: Transform Individual Labels to common Label Type

node.name: Model Writer (deprecated)
node.annotation: Write model to file for re-use in different workflow

node.name: Joiner (deprecated)
node.annotation: Join segmented image with input image

node.name: Segment Cropper
node.annotation: Parse related image segment

node.name: Color Manager
node.annotation: Choose colors for labels

node.name: Apply Color Settings To Labels
node.annotation: Choose segmented image to apply selected color

node.name: Interactive Segmentation View
node.annotation: View resulted segmented image applyed to input Image

annotations.annotation:
This workflow was built with KNIME Analytics Platform 4.0.2, however, similar workflows have been used with KNIME Analytics Platform 3.5 to 4.1 as well.
The sample images and pixel classification model are packaged with this workflow already, but can also be downloaded from https://doi.org/10.6084/m9.figshare.9936287.v2.
Instructions to follow before opening the workflow: 1) Follow instructions at https://knime.com/community/imagej to install the KNIME Image Processing - ImageJ Extension 2) Follow instructions at https://knime.com/wiki/knime-image-processing-nightly-build to add and activate the nightly software site of KNIME Image Processing 3) Go to File > Preferences > KNIME > Image Processing Plugin and add ",F" to the available dimension labels and restart KNIME Analytics Platform 4) Open the workflow and follow instructions to search and install missing extensions
If a model is available directly, use the Model Reader node instead of re-training a model.

metadata.author: helfrich
metadata.description:
DAB stain_AZ_TN
This workflow was built with KNIME Analytics Platform 4.0.2, however, similar workflows have been used with KNIME Analytics Platform 3.5 to 4.1 as well.
The sample images and pixel classification model are packaged with this workflow already, but can also be downloaded from https://doi.org/10.6084/m9.figshare.9936287.v2.
Instructions to follow before opening the workflow: 1) Follow instructions at https://knime.com/community/imagej to install the KNIME Image Processing - ImageJ Extension 2) Follow instructions at https://knime.com/wiki/knime-image-processing-nightly-build to add and activate the nightly software site of KNIME Image Processing 3) Go to File > Preferences > KNIME > Image Processing Plugin and add ",F" to the available dimension labels and restart KNIME Analytics Platform 4) Open the workflow and follow instructions to search and install missing extensions
